content:
# Particulate Matter

**Summary**: A mixture of solid and liquid particles suspended in the atmosphere.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016]] studied particulate matter (PM) to assess its impact on air quality.

- [[2019-alotaibi|Alotaibi, 2019]] studied particulate matter as a primary indicator of air quality.

- [[2024-alenazi|Alenazi, 2024]] measured particulate matter (PM) to assess air quality and health risks.

- [[2025-al-afif|Al-Afif, 2025]] monitored particulate-matter concentrations to assess health risks.

## Scientific Context

- [[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016]] PM is a major concern for human respiratory health and environmental impact.

- [[2019-alotaibi|Alotaibi, 2019]] studied particulate matter as a primary indicator of air quality.

- [[2024-alenazi|Alenazi, 2024]] measured particulate matter (PM) to assess air quality and health risks.

- [[2025-al-afif|Al-Afif, 2025]] monitored particulate-matter concentrations to assess health risks.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016]] Gravimetric analysis using an ECOTECH HiVol-3000 sampler.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Concentrations were 126.00 µg/m3, 177.00 µg/m3, and 379.85 µg/m3 in Dammam, Dhahran, and Khobar respectively. ([[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016, p. 14]])


- The study identified distinct differences between natural and man-made particulates across different regions. ([[2019-alotaibi|Alotaibi, 2019, p. 21]])

- The study measured both PM10 and PM2.5 concentrations inside buildings using a portable sensor. ([[2024-alenazi|Alenazi, 2024, p. 35]])

- PM10 levels remained consistently below 50 (Good) across all scenarios. ([[2025-al-afif|Al-Afif, 2025, p. 57]])
